title: Deep branch of medial circumflex femoral artery
summary: The deep branch runs obliquely upward upon the tendon of the obturator externus and in front of the quadratus femoris toward the trochanteric fossa, where it anastomoses with twigs from the superior gluteal artery and inferior gluteal artery. It primarily supplies blood to the posterior hip joint capsule and the adjacent musculature, and contributes to the trochanteric anastomosis.
text: The deep branch runs obliquely upward upon the tendon of the obturator externus and in front of the quadratus femoris toward the trochanteric fossa, where it anastomoses with twigs from the superior gluteal artery and inferior gluteal artery. It primarily supplies blood to the posterior hip joint capsule and the adjacent musculature, and contributes to the trochanteric anastomosis.
